Title: Your first save round trip
Summary: A guided tour of what happens between clicking Save in Bleepforge and seeing the change reflected in Godot.
Body:
## The trip end to end

Pick any item from [Items](/items) and change its Display name. When you click Save:

1. The client PUTs the JSON to `/api/items/<slug>`.
2. The server validates the body against the Item schema.
3. The JSON cache file at `data/items/<slug>.json` is overwritten.
4. The server resolves the matching `.tres` file under your Godot project, parses it, applies the JSON delta, and writes a temp file.
5. The temp file is renamed over the real `.tres`. This rename is atomic: there is no half written file.
6. The path is recorded in a self write list with a 1.5 second TTL so the file watcher does not bounce the change back as an external edit.
7. A save event flows out over Server Sent Events to every open browser tab. The [Saves tab](/diagnostics/saves) appends a row, and a toast pops in the corner if you started the change in another tab.
8. Open the file in Godot and see your new Display name.

## What can go wrong

If the `.tres` write fails (permissions, missing file, unexpected schema), the JSON cache is still updated. The save response carries a `tresWrite` block that the client logs to the console. Errors also appear in the [Logs tab](/diagnostics/logs).

> tip: open [Diagnostics → Saves](/diagnostics/saves) in a second tab. Every save you make and every Godot side save shows up there in real time, with direction badges.
